_meta.note: All sizes in MB unless stated. Calculations assume 64-bit system.

structureCosts.name: long long array
structureCosts.notes: Use when values exceed 2*10^9. Half the capacity of int array.

structureCosts.name: 2D int array [n][m]
structureCosts.notes: Safe up to ~4000x4000 in 64MB. Common DP table — check before declaring state.

structureCosts.name: Segment Tree
structureCosts.notes: Uses 4n space. For n=10^6 it is 16MB — very safe. For lazy propagation double it: 8n.

structureCosts.name: Segment Tree with Lazy Propagation
structureCosts.notes: Lazy doubles memory. At n=10^6 uses 32MB — still safe in 64MB.

structureCosts.name: Fenwick Tree (BIT)
structureCosts.notes: Same as int array. Most memory-efficient range structure. No overhead.

structureCosts.name: Sparse Table
structureCosts.notes: Uses n*log(n) space. Safe for n=10^5. At n=10^6 exceeds 64MB. Static only — no updates.

structureCosts.name: Adjacency List (graph)
structureCosts.notes: Standard graph representation. Memory proportional to edges. Use for sparse graphs.

structureCosts.name: Adjacency Matrix (graph)
structureCosts.notes: ONLY use for dense graphs with n <= 3000-4000. n=10^5 needs 40GB — never use.

structureCosts.name: Trie (26-char alphabet)
structureCosts.notes: Each node stores 26 child pointers (8 bytes each) + data. Very heavy per node. Use array-based Trie to reduce: ~4 bytes per child pointer = ~108 bytes/node.

structureCosts.name: Suffix Array + LCP
structureCosts.notes: Very memory efficient for string processing. 3 int arrays of size n.

structureCosts.name: 1D DP table
structureCosts.notes: Essentially free. Never a memory concern for 1D DP.

structureCosts.name: 2D DP table [n][m]
structureCosts.notes: Most common DP memory issue. Always check n*m before declaring 2D state.

structureCosts.name: 3D DP table [n][m][k]
structureCosts.notes: Dangerous. Even moderate dimensions explode. Always check before writing 3D DP.

structureCosts.name: Hash Map (unordered_map)
structureCosts.notes: Much heavier per entry than arrays. Use arrays when keys are bounded integers. Reserve size upfront to avoid rehashing.

structureCosts.name: Union Find (DSU)
structureCosts.notes: Two int arrays. Extremely memory efficient. Safe up to n=8*10^6 in 64MB.

decisionLayer.reason: Adjacency matrix for n>4000 exceeds 64MB. Use adjacency list instead.

decisionLayer.condition: n > 500000 AND structure = sparse_table AND memLimit = 64mb
decisionLayer.reason: Sparse table at n>500K exceeds 64MB. Use Segment Tree instead.
2D DP table n*m > 16M integers = 64MB. Check your memory limit.
decisionLayer.alternative: Consider space optimization — rolling array reduces to O(m) space

decisionLayer.reason: 3D DP table exceeds 16MB. Very likely to MLE on 64MB limits.
decisionLayer.alternative: Check if one dimension can be eliminated or compressed

decisionLayer.condition: n > 300000 AND structure = trie AND memLimit = 64mb
decisionLayer.reason: Standard Trie with n>300K nodes may exceed 64MB. Use array-based Trie.
Hash map has 50-100x overhead vs array. If keys are bounded integers, use direct array indexing.
decisionLayer.alternative: Direct array indexed by key value

decisionLayer.condition: n <= 4000000 AND structure = segment_tree AND memLimit = 64mb
decisionLayer.reason: Segment tree uses 4n ints. At n=4*10^6 it is 61MB — just fits.
If DP only reads from previous row, reduce 2D table to two 1D arrays.
decisionLayer.alternative: Two 1D arrays — reduces from O(n*m) to O(m) space

commonPatterns.name: 0/1 Knapsack space optimization
commonPatterns.optimized: O(W) space — single 1D array, iterate backwards
commonPatterns.when: Standard 0/1 knapsack where you only need current and previous row
commonPatterns.savings: Reduces memory by factor of n

commonPatterns.name: LCS / Edit Distance space optimization
commonPatterns.when: Only need the value, not the reconstruction path

commonPatterns.name: Implicit graph — no explicit storage
commonPatterns.naive: Store all edges explicitly — O(E) space
commonPatterns.optimized: Generate neighbors on the fly — O(1) extra space
commonPatterns.when: Graph is defined by a rule (grid adjacency, one edit away, etc.)

commonPatterns.name: Coordinate compression
commonPatterns.naive: Array indexed by value — O(max_value) space
commonPatterns.when: Values are large (up to 10^9) but count is small (n = 10^5)

commonPatterns.name: Bitset DP optimization
commonPatterns.when: DP transitions are OR/AND operations on boolean states
